name: Faker (Python Fake Data Generator)

agent_readiness.notes: Fake data generation for testing. from faker import Faker. fake = Faker(). fake.name() → 'John Smith'. fake.email() → 'john@example.com'. fake.address(). fake.phone_number(). fake.date_of_birth(). fake.uuid4(). fake.text(). fake.sentence(). fake.paragraph(). fake.company(). fake.job(). fake.url(). fake.ipv4(). fake.credit_card_number(). Locale support: Faker('de_DE'), Faker(['en_US', 'ja_JP']). Seeding: fake.seed_instance(42) for reproducible data. Providers: faker.providers.bank, faker.providers.color, etc. Custom providers: class MyProvider(BaseProvider). fake.profile() for complete person profile. fake.pydict(), fake.pylist() for Python objects. Used with factory_boy and pytest fixtures.

gotchas: Some locales have incomplete provider coverage
seed_instance() per instance vs Faker.seed() global — easy to confuse
Large install (100+ providers); consider faker-optional for selective install
Generated data is fake but not always realistic for edge cases
Not suitable for security-sensitive fake PII (use dedicated tools)
best_when: Generating realistic fake data for test fixtures
Database seeding with localized fake data
Factory-based test data with factory_boy integration
Generating bulk fake datasets for development environments
avoid_when: Reproducible exact data — use fixtures or real data snapshots
When mimesis performance matters (mimesis is faster)
summary: Faker generates realistic fake data for Python tests. fake = Faker(); fake.name(), fake.email(), fake.address(), fake.uuid4(). Locale support: Faker('de_DE'). Seeding: fake.seed_instance(42). 100+ providers. Custom providers via BaseProvider. fake.profile() for full person. MIT by joke2k. Best for test fixtures and DB seeding. Works with factory_boy. Mimesis is faster; factory_boy provides ORM integration.
